user.title: Connect to Eveus Charger
user.description: Enter your Eveus charger connection details.

soc.title: SOC Monitoring Setup
soc.description: Set your EV's battery capacity and charging efficiency loss. You can change these anytime from the entities later.

reconfigure.title: Update Eveus Charger
reconfigure.description: Update your Eveus charger connection details.

reauth_confirm.title: Update Eveus Credentials
reauth_confirm.description: The Eveus charger rejected the stored username or password. Enter the updated credentials.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to charger, please verify the IP address
error.invalid_input: Invalid input provided, please check your entries
error.invalid_device: Device validation failed, please check the charger model

abort.wrong_device: The credentials belong to a different charger

confirm.title: Update Eveus charger setup
confirm.description: The stored Eveus charger setup is incomplete or invalid. Enter the charger details again.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to charger, please verify the IP address
error.invalid_input: Invalid input provided, please check your entries
error.invalid_device: Device validation failed, please check the charger model

abort.entry_missing: The Eveus charger setup no longer exists

soc_dashboard_update.title: Eveus: update your SOC dashboard cards
soc_dashboard_update.description: Eveus now provides SOC inputs as native number entities. For dashboard cards and automations, replace the old helper prefix `input_number.ev_` with `number.eveus_`: `input_number.ev_initial_soc` -> `number.eveus_initial_soc`, `input_number.ev_target_soc` -> `number.eveus_target_soc`, `input_number.ev_battery_capacity` -> `number.eveus_battery_capacity`, and `input_number.ev_soc_correction` -> `number.eveus_soc_correction`. After no dashboard cards or automations reference `input_number.ev_*`, you can delete the old helpers.
